title: Cybersecurity and Privacy 
summary: The organization places a strong emphasis on building and maintaining customer and employee trust through robust systems and product security, cybersecurity, and privacy initiatives. A dedicated team is responsible for implementing a comprehensive range of technical and organizational security measures, which are supported by recognized certifications such as ISO/IEC 27001 (GB/T 22080-2016) and R155/R156. To further enhance its commitment to data privacy, the organization has launched a corporate-wide initiative that leverages cross-functional resources.

To manage enterprise vulnerabilities, the organization conducts regular security vulnerability scans, internal and external penetration testing, and adheres to established incident response protocols. A centralized view of information assets has been established to improve management and protection, complemented by cybersecurity risk monitoring policies designed to detect potential threats.

In terms of cloud security, the organization has developed policies aimed at securing its cloud architecture while extending security capabilities. This includes the use of signed certificates, encryption keys, and cryptographic algorithms to ensure product and software security, particularly for over-the-air (OTA) updates. The vehicle development process integrates a Threat Analysis and Risk Assessment (TARA) approach, enhancing resilience against potential cyberattacks.

Privacy policies are crafted to balance the beneficial use of data with customer privacy and legal compliance, guided by global privacy laws. The organization adheres to privacy-by-design principles in both software and hardware development, adapting its measures to address increased risks as the business scales. Collaboration between IT and legal functions is emphasized to ensure effective privacy management.

To foster a culture of cybersecurity and privacy awareness, the organization promotes education and training initiatives. Overall, the organization is committed to continuously evolving its security and privacy strategies by employing best practices and tailored risk management frameworks, ensuring a proactive approach to safeguarding both customer data and organizational integrity.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Inc. 
summary: L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (LCAA) has received approval from its board of directors for a Merger Agreement involving Lotus Technology Inc. (LTC) and its subsidiaries, Lotus Temp Limited and Lotus EV Limited. The agreement, which outlines a two-step merger process, was initially dated January 31, 2023, and amended on October 11, 2023. In the first step, Lotus Temp Limited will merge with LCAA, making LCAA a wholly-owned subsidiary of LTC, and converting its shareholders into LTC shareholders. The second step involves the surviving entity from the first merger merging into Lotus EV Limited, which will also become a wholly-owned subsidiary of LTC. 

Shareholders of LCAA will vote on the proposed Business Combination, which, if approved, will transition their shares to LTC shares. Key actions prior to the merger include the conversion of LTC's preferred shares into ordinary shares, the adoption of amended articles of association, and a recapitalization process that will affect the share structure and outstanding options. 

LTC, a Cayman Islands holding company, has undergone a restructuring to eliminate its previous variable interest entity (VIE) structure, now directly operating through its subsidiaries in China. This change presents certain risks, including regulatory challenges in China and potential trading prohibitions in the U.S. under the Holding Foreign Companies Accountable Act (HFCAA). The company plans to list its American Depositary Shares (ADSs) on Nasdaq following the merger.

The document also outlines the financial performance of LTC, including audited and unaudited financial statements, and details the registration fees associated with the securities being registered in connection with the merger. The extraordinary general meeting for LCAA shareholders will address the merger proposals, with the board recommending approval. However, shareholders are advised to consider potential conflicts of interest among LCAA's directors and officers regarding the Business Combination. The SEC has not evaluated the merits of the transactions, and any claims to the contrary are considered criminal offenses.

title: Lotus Technology Inc. (the "Company") 
summary: The Cayman Islands legal counsel has prepared a registration statement on Form S-8 for the Company, which will be filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on May 6, 2024. This statement involves the registration of 51,550,661 ordinary shares, each with a par value of US$0.00001, to be issued under the Company's 2022 Stock Incentive Plan. The legal opinion supporting this registration is based on a review of key documents, including the Registration Statement, the Plan, and the Company’s sixth amended and restated memorandum and articles of association, effective from February 22, 2024. 

The opinion asserts that the shares will be validly issued, fully paid, and non-assessable upon issuance and payment according to the Plan and relevant resolutions, with proper entries in the company's register of members. "Non-assessable" indicates that shareholders are not obligated to make further contributions to the Company's assets concerning the issued shares, except in exceptional circumstances such as fraud or illegal activities. 

The opinion also notes that the register of members serves as prima facie evidence under the Cayman Islands Companies Act, but does not reflect third-party interests and can be subject to rectification by court order in cases of fraud or error. The authors of the opinion clarify that it is based solely on the facts known at the time and does not extend to foreign laws. They rely on several assumptions regarding the authenticity of documents and the validity of resolutions without independent verification.

Additionally, the authors consent to the use of their opinion in the Registration Statement but do not consider themselves "experts" under the Securities Act. The correspondence concludes with a formal closing, "Yours faithfully," reflecting professionalism. The law firm involved, Maples and Calder (Hong Kong) LLP, is recognized for its expertise in corporate and commercial law, offering tailored legal services to a diverse clientele.

In this opinion letter, the phrase "non-assessable" means, with respect to the issuance of Shares, that a shareholder shall not, in respect of the relevant Shares, have any obligation to make further contributions to Lotus Technology's assets (except in exceptional circumstances, such as involving fraud, the establishment of an agency relationship or an illegal or improper purpose or other circumstances in which a court may be prepared to pierce or lift the corporate veil). These opinions are subject to the qualification that under the Companies Act (As Revised) of the Cayman Islands (the "Companies Act"), the register of members of a Cayman Islands company is by statute regarded as prima facie evidence of any matters which the Companies Act directs or authorizes to be inserted therein. A third party interest in the shares in question would not appear. An entry in the register of members may yield to a court order for rectification (for example, in the event of fraud or manifest error). These opinions are given only as to, and based on, circumstances and matters of fact existing and known to Lotus Technology on the date of this opinion letter.

title: 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. is a foreign company that files a FORM 6-K with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to provide updates on significant corporate events and financial performance. This report is essential for ensuring that U.S. investors have access to timely information about the company's operations, particularly as it pertains to its financial health and corporate governance.

For the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology reported substantial growth in key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. Deliveries increased by 239%, reaching 4,873 units, while revenues surged to $398 million, a 206% rise. Despite these gains, the company faced rising costs, leading to an operating loss of $438 million and a net loss of $460 million for the first half of 2024. The adjusted net loss also increased, reflecting ongoing financial challenges.

The company's balance sheet as of June 30, 2024, showed total assets rising significantly to $2.5 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. However, total liabilities also grew, resulting in a shareholders' deficit that improved but remained negative. Notably, the company eliminated mezzanine equity, indicating a shift in its capital structure.

Lotus Technology's product lineup includes several high-performance vehicle models, with projections indicating continued growth and profitability by 2026. The company anticipates producing over 30,000 units and achieving a gross profit margin exceeding 20%. Recent product launches, including limited-edition models, highlight the brand's focus on luxury and exclusivity.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates significant revenue growth and operational advancements, it continues to navigate challenges related to rising costs and net losses. The financial data presented underscores both the company's potential and the need for careful management of its financial resources as it aims for future profitability.

Deliveries increased by 239%, reaching 4,873 units, while revenues surged to $398 million, a 206% rise. However, total liabilities also grew, resulting in a shareholders' deficit that improved but remained negative. Notably, the company eliminated mezzanine equity, indicating a shift in its capital structure.

Lotus Technology's product lineup includes several high-performance vehicle models, with projections indicating continued growth and profitability by 2026. The company anticipates producing over 30,000 units and achieving a gross profit margin exceeding 20%. Recent product launches, including limited-edition models, highlight the brand's focus on luxury and exclusivity.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates significant revenue growth and operational advancements, it continues to navigate challenges related to rising costs and net losses. The financial data presented underscores both the company's potential and the need for careful management of its financial resources as it aims for future profitability.

For the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology reported substantial growth in key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. Deliveries increased by 239%, reaching 4,873 units, while revenues surged to $398 million, a 206% rise. Despite these gains, the company faced rising costs, leading to an operating loss of $438 million and a net loss of $460 million for the first half of 2024. The adjusted net loss also increased, reflecting ongoing financial challenges.

The company's balance sheet as of June 30, 2024, showed total assets rising significantly to $2.5 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. However, total liabilities also grew, resulting in a shareholders' deficit that improved but remained negative. Notably, the company eliminated mezzanine equity, indicating a shift in its capital structure.

Lotus Technology's product lineup includes several high-performance vehicle models, with projections indicating continued growth and profitability by 2026. The company anticipates producing over 30,000 units and achieving a gross profit margin exceeding 20%. Recent product launches, including limited-edition models, highlight the brand's focus on luxury and exclusivity.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates significant revenue growth and operational advancements, it continues to navigate challenges related to rising costs and net losses. The financial data presented underscores both the company's potential and the need for careful management of its financial resources as it aims for future profitability.

(“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, rang today’s opening bell at the Nasdaq stock exchange in New York City in celebration of its public listing. The Company’s American depositary shares (“ADSs”) commenced trading today on the Nasdaq Stock Market LLC (“Nasdaq”) under the ticker symbol “LOT” following the completion of its business combination on February 22, 2024, with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (“L CAA”), a special purpose acquisition company (“SPAC”) formed by affiliates of L Catterton, a leading global consumer-focused investment firm. Lotus Technology Inc. CEO Feng Qingfeng was joined by members of the Lotus Technology Inc. and L Catterton teams in New York City to witness this important milestone, which will help Lotus Technology Inc. to further its mission of becoming an advanced, fully electric, intelligent, and sustainable luxury mobility provider under the brand’s Vision80 strategy. “This is an exciting moment for all of us at Lotus Technology Inc.,” said Mr. Feng. “I would like to extend my appreciation to our shareholders, partners, suppliers, our outstanding employees, and most importantly, our customers, for their support.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Celebrates Public Listing
on Nasdaq 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. (Lotus Tech), a leading luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, marked a significant milestone by going public on February 23, 2024, with its shares trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker "LOT." This event followed its merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (LCAA), a SPAC linked to the global investment firm L Catterton. The public listing is a crucial step in Lotus Tech's ambition to become a leader in advanced, fully electric, and sustainable luxury mobility, aligning with its Vision80 strategy. CEO Feng Qingfeng celebrated the achievement alongside the L Catterton team, expressing gratitude to stakeholders and emphasizing the company's commitment to its heritage and future growth.

Chinta Bhagat, Co-CEO of LCAA, highlighted the potential for Lotus Tech to thrive in the expanding luxury electric vehicle market, aided by insights from their partnership with LVMH. The listing is expected to accelerate Lotus Tech's development of next-generation automotive technologies and expand its global distribution network from around 200 to over 300 stores by 2025. The company aims to be the first traditional luxury auto brand to offer a fully electric lineup by 2027, with plans to introduce additional high-performance models, including the Eletre hyper-SUV and Emeya hyper-GT, in the coming years. The celebratory bell-ringing ceremony was broadcasted from Times Square, featuring Lotus's signature battery electric vehicle models, allowing global employees to join in the festivities.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology Inc. is proud to carry Lotus’s heritage into the future and to open a new chapter in the Lotus story with our public listing today.” Chinta Bhagat, Co-Chief Executive Officer of L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp, said: “L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp believes that Lotus Technology Inc. is ideally positioned to seize the tremendous opportunity in the fast-growing and underserved luxury electric vehicle market, leveraging our global consumer insights and strategic relationship with LVMH. Today’s public listing is a significant step in Lotus Technology Inc.’s future expansion, and L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp is looking forward to continued collaboration with Lotus Technology Inc.’s talented management team.” The Nasdaq listing will support Lotus Technology Inc.’s development of next generation automobility technologies and the expected expansion of its global distribution network from around 200 to over 300 stores globally by 2025. Set to become the first traditional luxury auto brand to achieve a 100% electric product portfolio by 2027, Lotus Technology Inc. has launched a well-balanced portfolio of exceptionally performant models, including the Eletre hyper-SUV and Emeya hyper-GT, and plans to launch two additional luxury battery electric vehicles (BEV) over the next two years. The bell-ringing ceremony was broadcast on Nasdaq’s digital billboards in Times Square and livestreamed online, with Lotus Technology Inc. employees joining the celebration remotely from offices across the world. The brand’s signature battery electric vehicle models, Evija, Eletre, and Emeya, were also displayed in Times Square in front of the Nasdaq stock exchange.

title: Summary of LCAA’s Financial and Valuation Analysis 
summary: The summary provides an overview of the financial and valuation analyses conducted by the LCAA Board concerning the valuation of Lotus Tech in the context of a Business Combination. It underscores that the summary is not comprehensive and does not indicate the relative importance of the various analyses considered by LCAA's management. The Board may have prioritized certain assumptions over others, and the tabular data included should be interpreted within the broader financial analyses and methodologies to avoid misleading conclusions.

The valuation analyses were grounded in significant assumptions about market size, commercial efforts, industry performance, and economic conditions, many of which are beyond the control of LCAA, Lotus Tech, or other involved parties. Consequently, neither LCAA nor Lotus Tech can assure that future results will align with projections, as actual outcomes may differ significantly. The estimates provided do not represent actual market values or predict future trading prices for Lotus Tech shares post-transaction, and the financial analysis is subject to substantial uncertainty.

The LCAA Board utilized Comparable Company Analysis (CCA) to assess Lotus Tech's value by comparing it to similar companies in the Battery Electric Vehicle (BEV) and luxury Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) sectors. This method involves selecting peer companies based on shared characteristics, gathering financial data, calculating valuation multiples, and applying these multiples to estimate the target company's value while adjusting for differences in growth prospects and market conditions.

In the comparative analysis, the Board examined BEV companies such as Tesla, Lucid Group, Polestar, and NIO, alongside luxury OEMs like Ferrari, Porsche, and Aston Martin. The analysis highlighted similarities in innovation, sustainability, performance orientation, and market positioning between Lotus Tech and these companies. While Lotus Tech currently offers Internal Combustion Engine (ICE) models, it aims to transition to 100% BEV production by 2027, reflecting a broader industry trend toward sustainability and advanced technology.

The profitability profiles of emerging BEV companies reveal that they are currently operating at a loss, similar to Lotus Tech, which is still in the early stages of development compared to established luxury OEMs. These luxury manufacturers emphasize superior craftsmanship, innovative design, and brand prestige, targeting affluent consumers with a focus on performance and sustainability. Overall, the shared characteristics among luxury OEMs and Lotus Tech highlight a commitment to excellence and a distinctive market positioning within the automotive industry.

title: Inspection of Books and Records 
summary: The chapter on "Inspection of Books and Records" outlines the protocols established by the board of directors regarding shareholder access to the company's financial documents. The board holds the authority to define the conditions under which shareholders may inspect the company's accounts and books, including the extent of access, timing, location, and specific regulations governing such inspections. 

It is important to note that shareholders do not possess an inherent right to inspect company documents. Access is contingent upon either legal mandates, explicit authorization from the board, or approval through a special resolution passed by the shareholders. This framework ensures that the inspection process is regulated and aligned with both corporate governance and legal requirements.

In summary, the board of directors plays a crucial role in determining the parameters for document inspection, thereby safeguarding the company's interests while also adhering to applicable laws that may grant shareholders certain rights.

Under the Delaware General Corporation Law, any shareholder of Lotus Technology may for any proper purpose inspect or make copies of Lotus Technology’s stock ledger, list of shareholders, and other books and records. Shareholders of Cayman Islands exempted companies like Lotus Technology have no general right under Cayman Islands law to inspect corporate records (other than the memorandum and articles of association, the register of mortgages and charges, and any special resolutions passed by Lotus Technology's shareholders) or obtain copies of the list of shareholders of these companies. Under Cayman Islands law, the names of Lotus Technology's current directors can be obtained from a search conducted at the Registrar of Companies. However, Lotus Technology intends to provide its shareholders with annual reports containing audited financial statements.

metadata.title_summary:
title: ACCOUNTS, AUDIT AND ANNUAL RETURN AND DECLARATION 
summary: The Company's financial records will be maintained in a manner determined by the Directors, who have the discretion to change this approach as necessary. The books of account must be kept at the Registered Office or other suitable locations, and will be accessible for inspection by the Directors. Non-director Shareholders may inspect the Company's documents only under specific conditions set by the Directors, as there is no automatic right to do so unless permitted by law or through a Special Resolution.

The Company’s accounts will be audited based on the procedures and financial year-end established by the Directors; if no determination is made, the accounts will remain unaudited. The Directors have the authority to appoint an auditor, who will serve until removed by a Directors' resolution, and they also set the auditor's remuneration. Auditors have the right to access the Company's financial records and request information from the Directors and officers to perform their duties. They are required to report on the Company's accounts at the next annual general meeting following their appointment, and can provide additional reports as requested by the Directors or Shareholder meetings.

Furthermore, the Directors must prepare an annual return and declaration each year, containing information required by the Companies Act, which must be submitted to the Registrar of Companies in the Cayman Islands.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In the deposit agreement, all parties to the deposit agreement acknowledge that the DRS and Profile Modification System, or Profile, will apply to uncertificated American Depositary Shares (ADSs) upon acceptance thereof to the DRS by the Depository Trust Company (DTC). The DRS is the system administered by DTC pursuant to which the depositary may register the ownership of uncertificated ADSs, which ownership shall be evidenced by periodic statements issued by the depositary to the ADS holders entitled thereto. The Profile is a required feature of the DRS which allows a DTC participant, claiming to act on behalf of an ADS holder, to direct the depositary to register a transfer of those ADSs to DTC or its nominee and to deliver those ADSs to the DTC account of that DTC participant without receipt by the depositary of prior authorization from the ADS holder to register such transfer.
